title: synaedrys cuspidata
side_effects: Synaedrys cuspidata can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea and vomiting due to its emetic properties, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Prolonged use of the plant's extracts has been linked to liver and kidney damage, as well as increased risk of bleeding and bruising. In some cases, exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering. Additionally, improper use of Synaedrys cuspidata can also lead to respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath, in individuals with pre-existing respiratory conditions.
